questions.q: Nadir Shah, who invaded India in 1739, was the ruler of which country?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah was the ruler of Persia (Iran) when he invaded the Mughal Empire.

questions.q: In which year did Nadir Shah invade and sack Delhi?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah's invasion and sack of Delhi took place in 1739.

questions.q: Which empire did Nadir Shah invade in 1739?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah invaded the declining Mughal Empire in 1739.

questions.q: At which battle did Nadir Shah defeat the Mughal army?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah defeated the Mughal army at the Battle of Karnal in 1739.

questions.q: Which famous jewelled throne did Nadir Shah carry off from Delhi?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah carried off the Mughal Peacock Throne among his plunder.

questions.q: Which famous diamond did Nadir Shah take from Delhi in 1739?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah took the Koh-i-Noor diamond, along with the Peacock Throne, from Delhi.

questions.q: Nadir Shah's invasion is said to have accelerated what process for the Mughals?
questions.explanation: The invasion drained Mughal wealth and prestige, accelerating the empire's decline.

questions.q: The sack of Delhi by Nadir Shah involved widespread looting and what tragic event?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah ordered a massacre (qatl-e-aam) of Delhi's inhabitants amid the looting of the city.

questions.q: What did Nadir Shah primarily carry away from Delhi besides the throne and diamond?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah carried off immense treasure in gold, silver, and jewels from the Mughal capital.

questions.q: Nadir Shah's invasion entered India through which northwestern frontier region?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah advanced through Afghanistan and the northwest, crossing into the Punjab toward Delhi.

questions.q: Who was the Mughal emperor on the throne when Nadir Shah invaded in 1739?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Shah, often called 'Rangila', was the Mughal emperor defeated by Nadir Shah at Karnal.

questions.q: The Battle of Karnal was fought in present-day which Indian state?
questions.explanation: Karnal lies in present-day Haryana, north of Delhi, where the battle took place.

questions.q: The Battle of Karnal is noted for being decided in roughly what span of time?
questions.explanation: Despite the Mughals' larger numbers, the Battle of Karnal was decided in only a few hours.

questions.q: Before invading India, Nadir Shah had founded which Persian dynasty?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah founded the Afsharid dynasty of Persia after the fall of the Safavids.

questions.q: The Mughal general whose defection or hesitance is often cited at Karnal, later associated with negotiating with Nadir Shah, was which noble?
questions.explanation: Nizam-ul-Mulk, a leading Mughal noble, was involved in the negotiations with Nadir Shah after Karnal.

questions.q: The massacre Nadir Shah ordered in Delhi was reportedly triggered by what?
options: A rumour of his death and an attack on his troops
An eclipse seen as an omen
questions.explanation: A rumour that Nadir Shah had died sparked an uprising and killing of his soldiers, prompting his order for a massacre.

questions.q: After plundering Delhi, Nadir Shah reinstated which ruler on the Mughal throne as a vassal-like figure?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah restored Muhammad Shah to the Mughal throne after extracting treasure and territorial concessions.

questions.q: As a result of the invasion, Nadir Shah annexed Mughal territories west of which river?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah took the Mughal lands west of the Indus, including Kabul and the northwest.

questions.q: Nadir Shah's invasion exposed and worsened the weakness of the Mughals, opening the way for the rise of which Indian power in the following decades?
questions.explanation: Mughal weakness after 1739 helped the Marathas and other regional powers expand across India.

questions.q: A future founder of the Durrani Afghan empire served as a commander under Nadir Shah. Who was he?
questions.explanation: Ahmad Shah Abdali, later Ahmad Shah Durrani, served under Nadir Shah before founding the Durrani empire.

questions.q: The wealth Nadir Shah seized from Delhi was so vast that he reportedly did what for his Persian subjects?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah is said to have remitted taxes in Persia for a time owing to the enormous Indian plunder.

questions.q: Which northern Indian city, capital of the Mughals, was the principal target sacked by Nadir Shah?
questions.explanation: Delhi, the Mughal capital, was the city Nadir Shah sacked after his victory at Karnal.

questions.q: On approximately which date in 1739 was the Mughal army defeated at Karnal?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Karnal was fought on 24 February 1739, with Nadir Shah entering Delhi the following month.

questions.q: Nadir Shah's military success owed much to his effective use of which weapon, giving an edge over the Mughals?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah's forces used mobile light artillery, including camel-mounted swivel guns (zamburak), to great effect at Karnal.

questions.q: After Nadir Shah's death in 1747, into whose hands did the Koh-i-Noor pass first?
questions.explanation: After Nadir Shah was assassinated in 1747, the Koh-i-Noor passed to his general Ahmad Shah Durrani.

questions.q: The estimated plunder Nadir Shah carried from Delhi is often put at a figure of around what magnitude in the money of the time?
questions.explanation: Contemporary estimates of the plunder ran into the hundreds of millions of rupees, an immense sum for the era.

questions.q: Nadir Shah forced the Mughal emperor to cede Mughal control of which cities in the northwest?
questions.explanation: The treaty ceded Mughal lands west of the Indus including Kabul and Ghazni to Nadir Shah.

questions.q: How did Nadir Shah come to hold supreme power in Persia before his Indian invasion?
options: He acted as regent before crowning himself Shah
He ruled for the Mughal emperor
questions.explanation: Nadir rose as a military strongman acting nominally for the Safavids before crowning himself Shah of Persia in 1736.

questions.q: The contemporary Persian chronicle of Nadir Shah's campaigns, including the Indian invasion, was largely written by which court historian?
questions.explanation: Mirza Mahdi Khan Astarabadi, Nadir Shah's official historian, chronicled the campaigns in works such as the Tarikh-i-Nadiri.

questions.q: How long did Nadir Shah's occupation of Delhi roughly last before he withdrew to Persia?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah occupied Delhi for roughly two months in 1739 before withdrawing with his plunder to Persia.
